content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Both amendments identify the same verified inconsistency: run.json contains final_review_planner at lines 33-36 and 73-76, but the StageResolutionSnapshot struct (model.rs:186-207) has no such field and build_final_review_snapshot (engine.rs:8206-8223) only emits reviewers and arbiter. The stale data should be cleaned. Accepting both.

## Votes

- `fr-2-810dbb3f`: Accept
  Verified all three claims: (1) run.json contains final_review_planner at lines 33-36 (active snapshot) and 73-76 (last snapshot), (2) StageResolutionSnapshot at model.rs:186-207 defines no final_review_planner field — it only has final_review_reviewers and final_review_arbiter, (3) build_final_review_snapshot at engine.rs:8206-8223 constructs the snapshot with only reviewers and arbiter. Since serde lacks deny_unknown_fields on this struct, deserialization silently drops the stale field, but the persisted JSON is inconsistent with the current schema. Any downstream tool reading the JSON directly would see a phantom planner role. Regenerating the snapshot to match the current code is the correct fix.
- `fr-2-99c0c933`: Accept
  This amendment identifies the same verified inconsistency as fr-2-810dbb3f. The run.json file persists final_review_planner in both stage_resolution_snapshot and last_stage_resolution_snapshot, contradicting the current struct definition and builder function which have no planner field. The concern about reintroducing planner-specific state is valid — if this stale run.json is read by code that parses raw JSON (not via serde deserialization), or if it serves as a template for new runs, the phantom planner entry could propagate incorrect state. Cleaning the persisted data to match the code contract is warranted.
